properties.titulo_evento: Drone captures ongoing rescue efforts after Venezuela earthquakes
properties.descricao: Drone footage from Venezuela's La Guaira shows widespread destruction after twin earthquakes devastated the region.

properties.titulo_evento: Israel strikes Lebanon, testing days-old peace deal
properties.descricao: Hezbollah calls the deal a surrender as Israeli forces stay put and continue striking the south.

properties.titulo_evento: Pape: US trying to ‘squeak out of strategic defeat’ after Iran
properties.descricao: Political scientist Robert Pape argues the fallout of Iran war curbs US hubris and challenges Trump’s self-image.

properties.descricao: At least two killed in drone attacks, Russian authorities say, as Ukraine continues to target energy facilities.

properties.descricao: Rescuers in Venezuela are racing against time to find survivors after twin earthquakes left thousands missing.

properties.descricao: Israel is attacking West Bank agriculture in an attempt to drive Palestinian farmers off their land.

properties.descricao: Iran and the US trade attacks over navigation through the Strait of Hormuz, which has emerged as a major sticking point.

properties.titulo_evento: Satellite images show scale of destruction in Venezuela earthquakes
properties.descricao: Satellite images show the before and after of parts of Venezuela left in ruins by two devastating quakes.

properties.descricao: Days after Congress tells Trump he needs to seek its approval or end Iran war, Trump returns to attacks. Is it legal?

properties.titulo_evento: Two boys rescued from Venezuela earthquake rubble after days of being trapped

properties.titulo_evento: In Caracas, this feels like the hardest moment in Venezuela's modern history
properties.descricao: Rescue teams are working ceaselessly to reach those trapped under rubble. But as hope fades, anger is growing.

properties.titulo_evento: Europe's heatwave linked to 1,300 deaths, WHO says, as Germany hits record 41.7C
properties.descricao: WHO chief Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus warned that Europe is not prepared for high temperatures.

properties.descricao: Pride and a party as the Puerto Rican becomes the first Latin artist to headline a UK stadium.

properties.descricao: Two powerful earthquakes rocked Venezuela within seconds of each other on Wednesday, killing at least 920 people.

properties.titulo_evento: Rugby: Toulouse beats Montpellier to win fouth consecutive Top 14 title
properties.descricao: Toulouse clinched a ⁠fourth consecutive ​Top 14 title on Saturday with a 28-20 victory over Montpellier in ​the French championship final at the Stade de France, a match briefly halted for several minutes due to violent thunderstorms.

properties.titulo_evento: Russia's Putin promises security as Ukraine ramps up attacks
properties.descricao: The Russian leader condemned "terrorist attacks on our territory and infrastructure facilities." Ukrainian attacks on Russia's oil refineries and weapons factories have caused massive disruption in recent weeks.

properties.titulo_evento: Argentina: Milei's top aide resigns over corruption scandal
properties.descricao: Manuel Adorni is mired in a corruption scandal but has denied wrongdoing. Javier Milei has defended Adorni, saying, "Manuel is innocent."

properties.titulo_evento: Venezuela earthquakes: Nearly 7 million may be impacted — UN
properties.descricao: The projection includes up to two million people in the capital, Caracas. Rescue teams are racing against time to save survivors, three days after the twin quakes that killed 1,430 people. DW has more.

properties.titulo_evento: Ukraine strikes Russian weapons plant, Moscow fuel hub
properties.descricao: The two attacks, in Volgograd and Vladimir regions, are part of President Zelenskyy's strategy to bring the war to the Russian people and force Putin into peace negotiations.

properties.titulo_evento: Why is the US targeting Germany's drug industry?
properties.descricao: A new trade probe is reigniting a long-running clash over who should pay for medical innovation. Washington argues Germans benefit from lower prices while US patients shoulder a disproportionate share of the costs.

properties.titulo_evento: Rome celebrates 80 years of Italy's iconic Vespa scooter
properties.descricao: Thousands of Vespa riders took to Rome's streets to mark Vespa's 80th birthday. The wasp-waisted scooter became a symbol of Italy's rebirth after World War II.

properties.titulo_evento: Why is Germany taking a big stake in a major tankmaker?
properties.descricao: The German government will buy a 40% stake in tankmaker KNDS. The deal is as much about control in the critical defense sector as it is about the Franco-German relationship.

properties.titulo_evento: Why Germany's Deutsche Bahn will face delays for many years
properties.descricao: Delayed, unreliable and forced into long detours due to construction: Germany no longer sets the pace when it comes to rail travel — and this is unlikely to change anytime soon. The company is mired in a deep crisis.

properties.titulo_evento: Military-age Ukrainian men could lose EU protection
properties.descricao: The European Commission has suggested withdrawing protection status for military-age Ukrainian males. A number of EU countries support the move — and so does Kyiv.

properties.titulo_evento: Vatican accuses EU of double standards on war
properties.descricao: Doctrinal chief in Rome says the European Union applies international law selectively, sanctioning some military invasions but not others.

properties.titulo_evento: In Strait of Hormuz, Renewed Strikes Threaten to Set Back Shipping Recovery
